seoTitle: How to Create and Set Up a Sprint in ClickUp
metaDescription: Learn how to enable ClickUp's Sprint ClickApp, build a sprint folder, set point estimates, and track velocity with dashboard widgets.
intro: Running agile sprints in ClickUp requires enabling a specific ClickApp before the sprint-focused features become available. This guide covers turning on Sprints, creating a sprint folder with defined iteration timeframes, adding story point estimates to your tasks, and setting up rollover rules so unfinished work carries into the next cycle automatically.

steps.heading: Enable the Sprint ClickApp
steps.body: Open your workspace admin panel and verify the Sprint ClickApp is turned on before you start building sprint folders.

steps.heading: Create a Sprint Folder
steps.body: Right-click your target Space to create a new Sprint Folder and define your iteration timeframe, such as one, two, or four weeks.

steps.heading: Configure automated sprint settings
steps.body: Set up automated creation windows and enable rollover so unfinished tasks push into the next sprint cycle automatically.

steps.heading: Add estimation fields
steps.body: Customize your sprint list's columns to include Story Points or Time Estimates so you can gauge workload.

steps.heading: Move tasks into the active sprint
steps.body: Add tasks from your main backlog into the current sprint's column groupings.

steps.heading: Track velocity with dashboards
steps.body: Use Sprint Dashboard widgets to live-track your team's burn-down progress and historical velocity across cycles.

faq.question: Do I need to enable a special feature before using sprints?
faq.answer: Yes, the Sprint ClickApp needs to be turned on in your workspace admin panel before sprint folders and related features become available.

faq.question: What happens to unfinished tasks at the end of a sprint?
faq.answer: You can configure automatic rollover rules so incomplete backlog items push into the next active sprint cycle without manual re-entry.

faq.question: How long should a sprint last?
faq.answer: ClickUp lets you configure custom durations, commonly one, two, or four weeks, depending on what fits your team's cadence.

faq.question: Can I track team velocity over multiple sprints?
faq.answer: Yes, Sprint Dashboard widgets can show historical velocity and burn-down data to help with future planning.
